title: Two new Galileo satellites enter service
summary: Two new satellites have now officially joined Europe’s Galileo satellite navigation constellation, strengthening its capabilities, resilience and autonomy. Four Galileo First Generation satellites remain to be launched, with the next pair planned for the end of the year.

title: A Million-Panel Project
summary: Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
summary: Written by Lucy Thompson, Senior Research Scientist, University of New Brunswick, Canada Earth planning date: Friday, July 27, 2026 As an APXS uplink lead and strategic planner, I have the privilege of working with the rover engineers most days that I am on operations. title: NASA programs feel effects of workforce reductions
summary:
More than two dozen major NASA projects say they have been affected by the loss of a fifth of NASA’s civil servant workforce in the last year.
The post NASA programs feel effects of workforce reductions appeared first on SpaceNews.

summary:

title: Live coverage: SpaceX ready for 2nd attempt to launch Starship Flight 13 following post-abort engine work
summary: The flight comes as Tropical Storm Bertha is expected to make landfall in Texas on Thursday. Liftoff of the Starship Flight 13 mission is scheduled during a 90-minute window that opens at 5:45 p.m. CDT (6:45 p.m. EDT / 2245 UTC).

title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
summary: Continuing agency efforts to bring space closer to home, NASA+ is heading to more streaming platforms. On Thursday, NASA announced its programming is on Fire TV Channels. Fire TV customers can easily access this content by asking Alexa+ on compatible devices. Future programming on Fire TV may include science mission launches, a test flight for […]
